nodes.label: AI as clinical information source
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable or nonsensical responder
nodes.grounding_span: I use LLM such as ChatGPT to validate my decisions or seek another opinion on the topic

nodes.label: Confidentiality in AI use
nodes.grounding_span: I sometimes use LLM/AI to double check my clinical assessment but am always careful not to release patient identifiers

nodes.label: AI as independent verification vs blind following
nodes.grounding_span: I don't just blindly follow the AI's response in making those decisions

nodes.label: AI for documentation efficiency
nodes.label_negative: AI for complex or non-routine tasks
nodes.grounding_span: AI models can definitely take over that! Also for patient counselling, I see that AI models can be used for common routine consults

nodes.label: AI-generated content for patient education
nodes.grounding_span: using AI generated content instead of producing their own content shows they are less focused in putting in the time to provide patient care

nodes.label: Patient safety and quality of care
nodes.grounding_span: ensure the prescribed medications are indicated, effective, safe, at the right dose, and convenient for the patient

nodes.label: Patient confidentiality and privacy
nodes.grounding_span: I need to uphold patient confidentiality

nodes.label: Professional autonomy
nodes.grounding_span: I generally will do everything independently

nodes.label: Efficiency and speed in work
nodes.grounding_span: creates more work in an already busy work environment

nodes.label: Personalized patient care
nodes.grounding_span: less focused in putting in the time to provide patient care

nodes.label: Satisfaction when AI confirms clinical reasoning
nodes.grounding_span: I feel satisfied when AI produces an answer that matches one of my references/thought process

nodes.label: Frustration with AI's verbose responses
nodes.grounding_span: I get frustrated when AI produces a nonsensical answer or when it gives an unexpected answer

nodes.label: Disapproval of doctors using AI-generated content for patient education
nodes.grounding_span: I know some of the doctors I work with use AI generated content to explain a use of a medication (which I frown upon)

nodes.label: Cautious optimism about AI integration in pharmacy
nodes.grounding_span: I see AI getting more integrated in pharmacy like in other industries

nodes.label: Verification-oriented — cross-checks AI outputs against other sources
nodes.grounding_span: I generally will do everything independently but sometimes when there are conflicting information from different sources, I will use AI to verify my thought process

nodes.label: Domain-specific interaction pattern: specific question for work, back-and-forth for pleasure
nodes.grounding_span: For work, I tend to ask a specific question... For pleasure, yes I love back-and-forth conversations with LLM
